macro_segments.title: The Four Brothers Case: Distinguishing Between Achos Zekukaso and Assur Levatel Mitzvas Yabmin

micro_segments.title: Mishnah Setup: Four Brothers with Two Sisters and the Requirement for Chalitzah
Gemara Question: Why Four Brothers Rather Than Three?
The Distinction: Weaker Zika with Multiple Brothers Versus Problem of Dismissing the Mitzvah
Limiting the Concern: We Only Fear One Death, Not Two

macro_segments.title: Three Sisters with Two Brothers: The Case of Achos Chalutzaso and Chalitzah Pesulah

micro_segments.title: Setup: Three Sisters Fallen Before Two Brothers
The Distribution: One Brother Chalitza to First, Second Brother to Second, Both to Third
Sequential Deaths and the Middle Sister: Why She Needs Chalitzah from Both
Introduction to Chalitzah Pesulah: Weaker Zika Results in Weaker Chalitzah

macro_segments.title: The Principle of Chalitzah Pesulah and Chazor Al Kol Ha'achim

micro_segments.title: Defining Chalitzah Pesulah: Cases Where the Zika is Weakened
Tosafos Precision: Pesulah Requires Specific Weakening, Not Merely Impossible Yibum
Examples of Weakened Zika: Achos Zekukaso, Ba'alas Haget, Ba'alas Ma'amar
Why Multiple Chalitzos Needed: Each Brother Must Remove Their Own Zika

macro_segments.title: Rav vs Shmuel: Debate on Whether Multiple Chalitzos Are Required

micro_segments.title: Rav's Position: Chalitzah Pesulah Requires Chalitzah from All Brothers
Shmuel's Position: One Brother Can Do Chalitzah to All

macro_segments.title: Rav vs Shmuel: Debate on Whether Multiple Chalitzos Are Required (continued)

micro_segments.title: The Case of Achos vs Tzaros: Which Chalitzah is Stronger?
Shmuel's Reasoning: First-Rate Chalitzah Needed Only for Co-wives

macro_segments.title: Two Wives Co-wives Case: Demonstrating Chalitzah Strength Through Co-wife Freedom

micro_segments.title: Setup: Two Wives with Co-wives, Single Brother Surviving
Shmuel's Principle: Second-Rate Chalitzah Insufficient to Free Co-wives
Implications: Preference for First-Rate Chalitzah When Co-wives Present

macro_segments.title: The Principle of Chalitzah Pesulah and Chazor Al Kol Ha'achim (continued)

micro_segments.title: The Ironic Effect: Strengthening the Zika Also Weakens Chalitzah

macro_segments.title: Re-reading Shmuel: The Order of Chalitzos and the Problem of Tzaras Krovat Chalutzso

micro_segments.title: Gemara Challenge: Why Is Devorah Better Than Leah If Both Are Problematic?
Reinterpretation: Shmuel Discusses Which Order to Begin and End
If Begin with Sisters: Cannot Finish with Co-wives Due to Tzaras Krovat Chalutzso
If Begin with Co-wives: Can Finish with Either Due to Permitted Krovat Tzaras Chalutzso

macro_segments.title: Re-reading Shmuel: The Order of Chalitzos and the Problem of Tzaras Krovat Chalutzso (continued)

micro_segments.title: Back to Original Reading: Rav Ashi's Explanation of Why Devorah is Better

macro_segments.title: Rav Ashi's Resolution: Zika Not Strong Enough to Create Tzaras Ervah

micro_segments.title: The Zika Exists But Insufficient: Achos Zekukaso Cannot Make Tzara Full Ervah
Consequence: Chalitzah with Co-wife Works Better Than with Sister
Mishna's Teaching Aligned: Reflects Understanding That Tzara Issue is Lesser

macro_segments.title: Alternative View: Beis Shammai and Eliminating Tzaras Ervah Concerns

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Abba's Suggestion: Shmuel Follows Beis Shammai Who Permits Tzaros
Gemara Objection: If No Tzara Problem, Why Not Allow Yibum with Co-wife?
Compromise Explanation: Beis Shammai Permitted Tzaros, But Takkanah Requires Chalitzah
